title: Enrich scripts with telemetry and specs

blocks.content:
The **Discover & generate** workflow grounds your script in what the service actually does in Grafana Cloud. Assistant can combine metrics, logs, and traces to propose endpoints and load mix, then layer in OpenAPI or repository detail so requests, parameters, and checks look like production—not guesswork.

When Assistant quotes **RPS** (requests per second) and **p95** latency per endpoint, read them as “how busy” and “how slow at the tail” signals from your telemetry—not as targets you must match in the test unless you choose to. See **Terms used in this path** on the path cover if you need a refresher.

## Discover from a service name

To generate from telemetry, complete the following steps:

1. In **k6 Script Authoring**, choose **Discover & generate**.
2. Prompt with the **service name** you want to test—for example, `Generate a k6 script for the checkout service`.
3. When Assistant lists discovered endpoints with categories (read, write, internal or health) and signals such as RPS and p95 latency, reply with numbers, plain language (for example “only write operations”), or accept the suggested subset. Skipping health probes is often right so they do not dominate the scenario.
4. If you have an **OpenAPI** or **Swagger** spec, supply it after you confirm endpoints. You can attach a JSON or YAML file, paste the spec, give a **public URL**, or fetch a private URL through a connected **Infinity** data source (a Grafana plugin that reads JSON or YAML from URLs—see [Infinity data source plugin](https://grafana.com/docs/plugins/yesoreyeram-infinity-datasource/latest/) and the Script Authoring doc). Assistant uses the spec for parameters, bodies, auth, and `check()` ideas; missing spec areas stay as `// TODO:` for you to finish.
5. Alternatively, provide a **GitHub repository URL** as the primary context when telemetry is sparse—for example, `Generate a k6 script for https://github.com/my-org/checkout-service`. Assistant pulls route handlers, auth middleware, schemas, and tests from the repo to drive generation.

Treat Assistant output as **proposed** load: always validate that selected endpoints are safe to stress in your environment and that PII or destructive writes are excluded. When in doubt, start with read-only endpoints and low VUs.
In the next milestone, you paste or attach an existing k6 script so Assistant can analyze issues such as cardinality, missing checks, and invalid options for Grafana Cloud k6.
